title: elymus dentatus
intro: Elymus dentatus, also known as the toothed wheat grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Elymus dentatus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate stress. The herb can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some traditional practices, it is also used to make a tincture or dried powder for consumption.
